heading: Protection and advocacy of individual rights

L. 105–12section 11 of Pub. L. 105–12section 14401 of Title 42Apr. 30, 1997Apr. 30, 1997Apr. 30, 1997Apr. 30, 1997

children.label: A
children.text: need services that are beyond the scope of services authorized to be provided by the client assistance program under ; andsection 732 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: are ineligible for protection and advocacy programs under subtitle C of the Developmental Disabilities Assistance and Bill of Rights Act of 2000 [ et seq.] because the individuals do not have a developmental disability, as defined in section 102 of such Act []; and42 U.S.C. 1504142 U.S.C. 15002

children.label: ii
children.text: are ineligible for services under the Protection and Advocacy for Mentally Ill Individuals Act of 1986 ( et seq.) because the individuals are not individuals with mental illness, as defined in section 102 of such Act ().142 U.S.C. 1080142 U.S.C. 10802See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 2 Construction
children.text: This section shall not be construed to require the provision of protection and advocacy services that can be provided under the Assistive Technology Act of 1998 [ et seq.].29 U.S.C. 3001

children.heading: Appropriations less than $5,500,000
children.label: b Appropriations less than $5,500,000
children.text: For any fiscal year in which the amount appropriated to carry out this section is less than $5,500,000, the Commissioner may make grants from such amount to eligible systems within States to plan for, develop outreach strategies for, and carry out protection and advocacy programs authorized under this section for individuals with disabilities who meet the requirements of subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (a)(1).

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: A Technical assistance
children.text: For any fiscal year in which the amount appropriated to carry out this section equals or exceeds $5,500,000, the Commissioner shall set aside not less than 1.8 percent and not more than 2.2 percent of the amount to provide a grant, contract, or cooperative agreement for training and technical assistance to the systems established under this section.

children.heading: Grant for the eligible system serving the American Indian consortium
children.label: B Grant for the eligible system serving the American Indian consortium
children.text: For any fiscal year in which the amount appropriated to carry out this section equals or exceeds $10,500,000, the Commissioner shall reserve a portion, and use the portion to make a grant for the eligible system serving the American Indian consortium. The Commission shall make the grant in an amount of not less than $50,000 for the fiscal year.

children.heading: Allotments
children.label: 2 Allotments
children.text: For any such fiscal year, after the reservations required by paragraph (1) have been made, the Commissioner shall make allotments from the remainder of such amount in accordance with paragraph (3) to eligible systems within States to enable such systems to carry out protection and advocacy programs authorized under this section for individuals referred to in subsection (b).

children.heading: Population basis
children.label: A Population basis
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), from such remainder for each such fiscal year, the Commissioner shall make an allotment to the eligible system within a State of an amount bearing the same ratio to such remainder as the population of the State bears to the population of all States.

children.heading: Minimums
children.label: B Minimums
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations to carry out this section, and except as provided in paragraph (4), the allotment to any system under subparagraph (A) shall be not less than $100,000 or ⅓ of 1 percent of the remainder for the fiscal year for which the allotment is made, whichever is greater, and the allotment to any system under this section for any fiscal year that is less than $100,000 or ⅓ of 1 percent of such remainder shall be increased to the greater of the two amounts.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For the purposes of paragraph (3)(B), Guam, American Samoa, the United States Virgin Islands, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands shall not be considered to be States.

children.heading: Allotment
children.label: B Allotment
children.text: The eligible system within a jurisdiction described in subparagraph (A) shall be allotted under paragraph (3)(A) not less than $50,000 for the fiscal year for which the allotment is made.

children.heading: Adjustment for inflation
children.label: 5 Adjustment for inflation
children.text: For any fiscal year, beginning in fiscal year 1999, in which the total amount appropriated to carry out this section exceeds the total amount appropriated to carry out this section for the preceding fiscal year, the Commissioner shall increase each of the minimum grants or allotments under paragraphs (1)(B), (3)(B), and (4)(B) by a percentage that shall not exceed the percentage increase in the total amount appropriated to carry out this section between the preceding fiscal year and the fiscal year involved.

children.heading: Proportional reduction
children.label: d Proportional reduction
children.text: To provide minimum allotments to systems within States (as increased under subsection (c)(5)) under subsection (c)(3)(B), or to provide minimum allotments to systems within States (as increased under subsection (c)(5)) under subsection (c)(4)(B), the Commissioner shall proportionately reduce the allotments of the remaining systems within States under subsection (c)(3), with such adjustments as may be necessary to prevent the allotment of any such remaining system within a State from being reduced to less than the minimum allotment for a system within a State (as increased under subsection (c)(5)) under subsection (c)(3)(B), or the minimum allotment for a State (as increased under subsection (c)(5)) under subsection (c)(4)(B), as appropriate.

children.heading: Reallotment
children.label: e Reallotment
children.text: Whenever the Commissioner determines that any amount of an allotment to a system within a State for any fiscal year described in subsection (c)(1) will not be expended by such system in carrying out the provisions of this section, the Commissioner shall make such amount available for carrying out the provisions of this section to one or more of the systems that the Commissioner determines will be able to use additional amounts during such year for carrying out such provisions. Any amount made available to a system for any fiscal year pursuant to the preceding sentence shall, for the purposes of this section, be regarded as an increase in the allotment of the system (as determined under the preceding provisions of this section) for such year.
have in effect a system to protect and advocate the rights of individuals with disabilities;

have the same general authorities, including the authority to access records and program income, as are set forth in subtitle C of title I of the Developmental Disabilities Assistance and Bill of Rights Act of 2000 [ et seq.];42 U.S.C. 15041

have the authority to pursue legal, administrative, and other appropriate remedies or approaches to ensure the protection of, and advocacy for, the rights of such individuals within the State or the American Indian consortium who are individuals described in subsection (a)(1);
provide information on and make referrals to programs and services addressing the needs of individuals with disabilities in the State or the American Indian consortium;

children.label: A
children.text: the objectives and priorities for the activities of the system for each year and the rationale for the establishment of such objectives and priorities; and

children.label: B
children.text: the coordination of programs provided through the system under this section with the advocacy programs of the client assistance program under , the State long-term care ombudsman program established under the Older Americans Act of 1965 ( et seq.), the Developmental Disabilities Assistance and Bill of Rights Act of 2000 [ et seq.], and the Protection and Advocacy for Mentally Ill Individuals Act of 1986 ( et seq.);section 732 of this title42 U.S.C. 300142 U.S.C. 1500142 U.S.C. 108011
establish a grievance procedure for clients or prospective clients of the system to ensure that individuals with disabilities are afforded equal opportunity to access the services of the system; and

provide assurances to the Commissioner that funds made available under this section will be used to supplement and not supplant the non-Federal funds that would otherwise be made available for the purpose for which Federal funds are provided.

children.heading: Direct payment
children.label: 1 Direct payment
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Commissioner shall pay directly to any system that complies with the provisions of this section, the amount of the allotment of the State or the grant for the eligible system that serves the American Indian consortium involved under this section, unless the State or American Indian consortium provides otherwise.

children.heading: Carryover
children.label: 2 Carryover
children.text: Any amount paid to an eligible system that serves a State or American Indian consortium for a fiscal year that remains unobligated at the end of such year shall remain available to such system that serves the State or American Indian consortium for obligation during the next fiscal year for the purposes for which such amount was paid.

children.heading: Limitation on disclosure requirements
children.label: h Limitation on disclosure requirements
children.text: For purposes of any audit, report, or evaluation of the performance of the program established under this section, the Commissioner shall not require such a program to disclose the identity of, or any other personally identifiable information related to, any individual requesting assistance under such program.

children.heading: Administrative cost
children.label: i Administrative cost
children.text: In any State in which an eligible system is located within a State agency, a State may use a portion of any allotment under subsection (c) for the cost of the administration of the system required by this section. Such portion may not exceed 5 percent of the allotment.

children.heading: Delegation
children.label: j Delegation
children.text: The Commissioner may delegate the administration of this program to the Commissioner of the Administration on Developmental Disabilities within the Department of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: Report
children.label: k Report
children.text: The Commissioner shall annually prepare and submit to the Committee on Education and the Workforce of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Labor and Human Resources of the Senate a report describing the types of services and activities being undertaken by programs funded under this section, the total number of individuals served under this section, the types of disabilities represented by such individuals, and the types of issues being addressed on behalf of such individuals.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: l Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $17,650,000 for fiscal year 2015, $19,013,000 for fiscal year 2016, $19,408,000 for fiscal year 2017, $19,838,000 for fiscal year 2018, $20,305,000 for fiscal year 2019, and $20,735,000 for fiscal year 2020.

children.heading: Eligible system
children.label: 1 Eligible system
children.text: The term “eligible system” means a protection and advocacy system that is established under subtitle C of the Developmental Disabilities Assistance and Bill of Rights Act of 2000 [ et seq.] and that meets the requirements of subsection (f).42 U.S.C. 15041

children.heading: American Indian consortium
children.label: 2 American Indian consortium
children.text: The term “American Indian consortium” means a consortium established as described in section 142 of the Developmental Disabilities Assistance and Bill of Rights Act ().142 U.S.C. 6042
